steps.title: Identify the competing mechanisms
steps.content: Text 1 concludes that flamingo coloring is determined by shrimp consumption. Text 2 describes an alternative mechanism: active pigment application through grooming. The key signal is that Text 2 reveals a second pathway to pink coloring that Text 1's author overlooks.

steps.title: Match to the answer
steps.content:
By pointing out that the amount of shrimp eaten isn't the only thing that influences flamingos' coloring captures the ecologist's rebuttal: shrimp consumption isn't the complete picture for flamingo coloring.
The other three options discuss feather location, perception, and diet timing rather than addressing whether shrimp consumption alone explains coloring.
That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
